verdict.tenant_notice_days: Written notice must be received by Landlord on or before May 31, 2027

There is no provision requiring the Landlord to notify or remind Tenant in advance of this deadline; the same pattern (tenant-only notice deadline, no landlord reminder obligation) appears identically in the Third Amendment's renewal option as well.

verdict.landlord_notice_window: Landlord must notify Tenant of its failure to exercise; Tenant then has 30 days after receipt of Landlord's notice to send its extension notice
verdict.provision_quote: "if Tenant shall fail to give notice to Landlord of Tenant's election to extend the term of this Lease for any of the aforesaid renewal terms, Landlord shall so notify Tenant, in writing, of Tenant's failure and subject to the terms of Section 2B, Tenant shall then have the right to send its written notice extending the term of this Lease at any time within thirty (30) days of receipt of Landlord's notice."
verdict.tenant_notice_days: At least twelve (12) months before the beginning of the extension period (per Section 2B)
verdict.note: This is a landlord notice-back/no-forfeiture provision: if Tenant fails to timely exercise its renewal option, Landlord is obligated to notify Tenant of that failure, and Tenant then gets a fresh 30-day window to exercise. If Landlord also fails to give this notice, the lease automatically extends year-to-year until either party gives notice, subject to an outside cap at the 30th anniversary of the Opening Date.

verdict.landlord_notice_window: Tenant's option right continues until 30 days after Landlord gives notice of election to terminate the option (no fixed advance window before the exercise deadline; instead the deadline is excused until Landlord acts)
verdict.tenant_notice_days: not less than six months prior to the then current expiration date of the term
verdict.provision_quote: "Each such option shall be exercisable by Tenant's giving written notice to Landlord of Tenant's intention to exercise the same not less than six months prior to the then current expiration date of the term; provided, however, that, if Tenant shall fail to give any such notice within the aforesaid time limit, Tenant's right to exercise its option shall nevertheless continue until 30 days after Landlord shall have given Tenant notice of Landlord's election to terminate such option and Tenant may exercise such option at any time until the expiration of said 30-day period... if Tenant shall fail to give notice to Landlord of Tenant's election to extend the term for any of the aforesaid extended terms and if Landlord shall fail to give notice to Tenant of Landlord's election to terminate Tenant's right to extend the Lease under the option applicable thereto, then...the term shall be automatically extended from year to year..."
verdict.section: 2006 Amendment to Lease, Paragraph 4 (Extension of Term, Article 2)
verdict.note: This is a landlord notice-back provision: if Tenant misses its own 6-month notice deadline, Tenant's option right survives (and the lease term auto-extends year to year) until Landlord affirmatively sends Tenant a notice electing to terminate the option, after which Tenant still gets a further 30 days to exercise. Thus the Tenant's failure to timely exercise is excused unless/until Landlord sends this notice, effectively obligating Landlord to notify Tenant before the option right can be cut off.

This functions as a landlord notice-back provision protecting Tenant's option from lapsing due to inadvertent failure to timely exercise. The same structure applies to the 'Extra Period' option under this section.

There is no provision requiring the Landlord to send a reminder or notice-back to Tenant before the exercise deadline; the risk of forfeiture for failing to timely exercise falls entirely on Tenant.

verdict.landlord_notice_window: Landlord must notify promptly after tenant's silence, then tenant gets 10 additional days after receipt to decline
verdict.provision_quote: If Tenant fails to give such notice, Landlord shall promptly notify Tenant that the Term will be extended, including the starting and ending dates of such Renewal Period and Tenant shall have an additional period of ten (10) days after receipt of such notice in which to give Landlord written notice of its election not to exercise such renewal option.
verdict.tenant_notice_days: 180 days prior to expiration of Term/Renewal Period (to decline renewal, since renewal is automatic)
verdict.note: Renewal is automatic unless Tenant affirmatively opts out at least 180 days before expiration. If Tenant fails to give that opt-out notice, Landlord is obligated to promptly notify Tenant that the Term will be extended, and Tenant then gets an additional 10-day window after receiving that notice to still decline. This is a landlord notice-back obligation that effectively extends/protects the tenant's decision window.

verdict.tenant_notice_days: 365 days prior to expiration of the then-current Lease Term
verdict.provision_quote: "Tenant shall exercise its option to extend for the applicable Option Term by giving Landlord written notice thereof (the "Option Notice") at least three hundred sixty-five (365) days prior to the expiration of the then-current Lease Term ... and if such Option Notice is not given timely, then Tenant's right to extend with respect to the applicable Option Term and any subsequent Option Term shall lapse and be of no further force or effect"
verdict.note: The lease places the notice obligation entirely on the Tenant, who must proactively send the Option Notice 365 days before expiration. There is no provision requiring the Landlord to remind or notify Tenant in advance of the option deadline; failure by Tenant to timely give notice causes an automatic, unexcused lapse of the renewal right.

verdict.landlord_notice_window: Landlord's notice can be sent at any time during the final Lease Year of the Term; Tenant's exercise deadline extends to 15 days after Tenant's receipt of such notice
verdict.provision_quote: "Tenant must deliver written notice to Landlord of its election to extend the Term of this Lease for an Extension Term on or before the later to occur of the following ... dates ...: (1) the date that is two hundred seventy (270) days before the expiration of the then expiring Term, ... or (2) the date that is fifteen (15) days after Tenant's receipt of notice from Landlord that Tenant has an option available to exercise (which notice from Landlord can be sent at any time during the final Lease Year of the Term). If the Term expires before such notice from Landlord, and Tenant continues in possession of the Premises after such expiration, the Term will be extended automatically on a month to month basis until fifteen (15) days after the date Landlord has given such notice, or Tenant surrenders the Premises to Landlord, or Tenant exercises its option..."
verdict.tenant_notice_days: 270 days before expiration of the then-expiring Term, or 15 days after receipt of Landlord's reminder notice, whichever is later
verdict.note: The lease sets a primary tenant deadline of 270 days before Term expiration, but if Landlord fails to send a reminder notice during the final Lease Year, Tenant's exercise window is extended to 15 days after Landlord eventually gives such notice, and possession continues month-to-month until that notice is given. This effectively obligates Landlord to notify Tenant of the option or risk an extended/holdover period, making failure to exercise excused until Landlord's notice.

verdict.tenant_notice_days: 270 days prior to expiration of the then effective Lease Term
verdict.provision_quote: Tenant shall be entitled to extend the then effective Lease Term for the Renewal Term(s), if any, set forth under Article 1 hereof, upon giving Landlord written notice, delivered not less than two hundred seventy (270) days prior to the expiration of the then effective Lease Term, time being of the essence, of Tenant's exercise of its renewal rights.
verdict.note: The lease only imposes a notice obligation on the TENANT (270 days prior notice to exercise renewal, time of essence) with no corresponding landlord duty to remind or notify Tenant of the upcoming deadline. No provision found requiring Landlord to send a reminder/notice-back before the option window closes.
